title: HIC Harold Import Co HIC Mortar and Pestle Set, 5.75-Inch, Tall, Marble
description:
About this item
HIC Kitchen’s Mortar and Pestle Set grinds spices, herbs, nuts, and more into a fine paste or powder
Made from solid fine-quality Carrara marble; complements any decor; mortar 4 x 4-inches; pestle 5 x 1.25-inches
Quickly grind and break down ingredients; speeds up food preparation with minimum effort
Make aromatic dry rubs, curry pastes, pestos, nut butters, ground cinnamon or grind peppercorns, prepare holistic remedies, pulverize pills, and more
The natural marble stone gives each piece a unique color and marble pattern; easy to use and clean; hand wash in warm, soapy water
reviews_5s:
I got this as a gift for my mom, she had been pointing out wooden ones we saw at the farmers market. I decided to give this one a try as it matched the counter tops. Nothing was missing, nothing was broken, it was heavier so it didn’t scoot across the table easily. So far I think it’s a good investment, and it might be worth giving a shot!Works great, is heavy and stays where you put it while grinding. Good buy!I bought this to grind my Celtic sea salt, when it arrived unfortunately the pestle was broken in two… I went to start the return and was informed they would simply send a new one, no need to return. I had a brand new set a few days later and now have a ‘decorative bowl’ for my shelf as well. Love a seller that makes these unfortunate instances easy. Used the new mortar and pestle set to grind my salt the next day and it worked wonderfully!!Looks great! Easy to use, durable! It’s as heavy as marble should feel, and is smooth on the outside and a little rough on the insideWhere has this thing been all my life...?? I am a Pink Mediterranean Sea Salt Junkie... lol And this is the perfect Grinder for it. I buy it coarse & grind it up into all different kinds of textures. It really brings out that gentle crunchiness Pink Sea Salt brings to the table.

I've seen the mixed reviews here, & if you feel yours really was not made right, I would definitely send it back for an exchange. Mine didn't arrive perfect, because these are hand crafted, but it didn't arrive with the glaring defects in workmanship that others are experiencing. If it doesn't look right, you should be able to exchange it for one that is. Mine looks, operates, & fits together fine.

Some people are concerned that they are eating finely ground marble along with their salts or spices. I see what they mean, but there's probably actually more of that same material in my Sea Salts, than is coming off of my Grinder. According to the Wikipedia, "Limestone is a sedimentary rock, composed mainly of skeletal fragments of marine organisms such as coral, forams, and molluscs. Its major materials are the minerals calcite and aragonite, which are different crystal forms of calcium carbonate (CaCO3)." Calcium Carbonate is commonly used in Calcium supplements & dietary antacid tablets. So like Sea Salts...it is organic.

If you don't like the texture of your ground spices or salts, practice with it. You'll get better at using it over time. When you grind, work things around in small circles around the curve of the bowl. The more I experiment with mine, I get a better feel for it, and the nicer & more interesting my textures are coming out.

The one con I have for this item is that it arrived with a major crack in the marble that runs down one side. It is otherwise well-made, but I hope it does not crack all the way across & break apart over time. I'm not exchanging it because other than this it is well-made.Crushes up pills perfectly and easy to use. It's a plus that it's so pretty too. I keep it on my counter in a little vignette on a wooden lazy susan with my salt and pepper shakers and a little faux plant. Looks so nice!The first mortar had a broken piece. I do appreciate that the replacement came in so fast in fact I was surprised. The mortar works and looks fantastic.Why don’t you have one of these the quality sold every kitchen needs onePurchased one at a fancy cookware store a year ago and it broke. This one is larger, better quality and half the price. If you need one it is worth it.I bought this to crush up medicated dog food that is too large for her to chew. Im pleasantly surprised. Its beautiful and looks great as a display.
reviews_1s:
The mortar is grooved with a crater in the middle that the pestle can't even get into so you end up with chunks you have to dig out and put on the side of the mortar somewhere. After that your have to keep it straight up and not try to use the pestle at an angle because if doesn't actually hit the side at that point. When you use the set, if any part of the pestle rubs into the mortar a fine stone dust is created so you end up with stone dust in whatever you are crushing. The only way to fix this is to get sandpaper and sand down the inside of the mortar, which is a pain. I only read the reviews of this product AFTER I had it and used it. I noticed a good number of people who have this same issue.

When I first received the set I was inspecting it for damage and found the polish on the outside was chipping off of the pestle and some was overhanging the side. I had to go over the rim of the pestle and chip off all of the sharp edges of the pestle. With this finally done I tried moving the pestle around in the mortar and it would bind often and if you are really trying to get a fine consistency it doesn't seem like the pestle even hits the bottom of the mortar. The sides of the pestle hit more then the rest of it.

One of the points in the description states "Closely fitted design, with smooth action and optimized grinding surface helps speed up food preparation and minimize effort." This is an absolute falsehood other than the closely fitted design part. The action is anything but smooth and the grinding surface of the pestle does not actually hit everywhere in the mortar.

If you like to modify the things you buy so they work correctly this is a great buy. But, if you would like the things you buy to work out of the box as designed and advertised I would stay away from this one.I purchased this on 10/30/24. Two days ago my wife was grinding pills for our dog and the mortar broke in her hand. I tried to reach out to the manufacturer and the email listed did not exist. My Amazon window ended on 11/30, about a week before this broke. This was clearly defective. Do not buy one!Broken pestle… set Was awful and unable to be usedThere are a bunch of mushroom mortar and pestles available. I've bought Harold Imports products in the past and had good experiences, and theirs had one of the lowest prices, so I gave it a try. I wasn't impressed.

First, there were a couple of chips in the pestle, in the polished surface at the base of the handle. Second, the marbles used weren't complimentary. The veining on both parts was similar, but the background color was way off. The mortar was more beige, and the pestle was more gray. I didn't need them to be exact matches, but I wanted them to look like they belonged together, and they didn't. Lastly, the finishing just wasn't that great, and the pestle wasn't smooth.

I returned it the next day.I bought two different name brands. This brand has a very poor quality bowl, the bowl its self is not concentric enough to use, it has a small high spot in the center of the bowl, making it useless. A waste of money.Recibí el producto rotoI bought this to grind fennel seed, thinking the large surface area would make it quick. There was only a small portion of the bowl that made contact with the pestle, so it took forever to grind a tiny amount. I thought the fat handle on the pestle would be easy to hold onto to apply pressure, but unfortunately it was not. I returned the item.Just opened it up and the pestle was broken in half. I will try taping it together. Seems a little flimsy. Look at other products before purchasing this one.Broken during second use and within days of purchaseNot good
